description: Okay, nice. Seems like you got some of the ideas right. Let's... You can just get rid of the album art, I think, entirely on a particular item. Because we won't really end up having that. And then I think it could just be a transcription of the text. So the text could be there, and then we could just have it play. So get rid of the album art and the title for a particular item. Also, let's bring down the question name. Let's bring it down a little lower. So that we can see it clearly on the page. Also, I don't think you need to change the background for the question. The question can just be the same background color. Thank you.

results.identified_issue: Four asks from the voice note, all in Discover. (1) The clip page was NowPlayingContent ported wholesale from keepMovin, so it drew a 300pt artwork square — always the placeholder chat bubble, since an argument has no cover art — plus a title that was really just clip.sideLabel. Removed both and made the transcript (clip.answerText, which is the dictated text of the recording) the body of the page. That let a lot of NowPlayingContent go with them: CachedAsyncImage, title/subtitle, the interactive BufferedScrubber (dead — Discover bans scrubbing, so the only caller always passed sliderInteractive: false), scrollable/showDescription. ArgumentClip.title was left dead, so I removed it. (2) Deliberately did NOT make the transcript a ScrollView: the feed is a paged vertical ScrollView and an inner scroll would swallow the swipe that changes pages. It uses minimumScaleFactor(0.5) to fit instead — if someone submits a very long answer the text may end up small, which is the thing to revisit first if this looks wrong. (3) The question was the inline toolbar principal item, which is why it truncated to one line against the status bar; it is now a header view above the feed inside a VStack, so the pages' containerRelativeFrame sizes to the reduced height and nothing overlaps. (4) QuestionIntroCard's SteelmanTheme.accent fill became Color(.systemBackground), white text became primary/secondary. Not built or run — vigil builds from the pushed commit — so this is verified by reading, not by seeing it on the device.
results.detailed_description: All four things from your note are in. An individual argument page no longer has album art or a title — the placeholder square and the side label are both gone, and the page is now the transcript of what the person said, with the play/skip-back controls and the position bar under it. The words on screen are the words being played, whether that is recorded audio or the text read aloud. Since the artwork and title slots are gone, I also stripped the leftover pieces of the player chrome that only existed to serve them, including a scrubber that was already disabled everywhere because Discover doesn't allow scrubbing. One thing to watch: the transcript shrinks to fit the page rather than scrolling, because an inner scroll view would eat the swipe that moves you to the next argument. For a short answer it reads fine; a very long one will get small, and if that bothers you I'd rather solve it with a follow-up than by breaking the swipe. The question name moved out of the navigation bar, where it was jammed against the status bar and cut off after one line, into a header just below it that has room for three lines — so you can actually read the question you're arguing about. And the question card no longer paints itself accent blue; it sits on the same background as every other page, with the big serif question and the read-aloud doing the work the colour was doing. I have not run this on the device — vigil builds from this commit — so it is worth a look at how the transcript sizes on a real answer.
results.summary: Argument pages now show the transcript instead of album art and a title; the question moved down out of the toolbar and the question card is back on the normal background
